title: Building a Modern Bank
description: What it takes to build a modern bank: the engineering, architecture and culture choices behind LHV Bank's platform.

title: Leading Through the Noise: Making AI Stick in Engineering Teams
description: AI is moving faster than most engineering organisations can track. How leaders can cut through the noise and drive adoption that actually sticks, through culture, experimentation and clear decisions.

title: Nimble scaling: LHV Bank accelerates value flow and strategic alignment through Team Topologies
description: How we restructured LHV Bank's engineering organisation for flow using Team Topologies. Hands-on workshops, captured as a Conflux case study.

title: AI, Data & Automation: Redefining the Customer Experience
description: What AI, data and automation actually change about customer experience in financial services.

title: WealthTech Panel Discussion
description: Panel on innovation, opportunities and challenges in WealthTech, alongside other sector leaders.

title: Scaling Panel Discussion
description: Panel on scaling fintechs, chaired by Julia Streets, covering growth strategies and the problems that come with them.

title: People as Catalyst and Obstacle to Change
description: Panel discussion on how people can be both the catalyst and the biggest obstacle to organisational change.

title: Leading with Cognitive Empathy
description: How cognitive empathy, using our own experiences to understand others, can help leaders build a better workplace. Followed by a panel with Stephanie B. and Lour Nader.

title: Demystifying Neurodiversity for Engineering Leaders
venue: WRK Digital Meetup, xDesign HQ Leeds
description: Co-hosted with Lindsay Baker, exploring what engineering leaders need to understand about neurodiversity in the workplace.

title: Build, Buy or Outsource? The Software Dilemma
venue: Fruition Consulting & Enablis, Brew Society Leeds
description: Debating build vs buy vs outsource with Cassy Calvert, Rebecca Fitzgerald, Paul Durkan and Ngaire Guzzetti.

title: React Context API – Do You Really Need Redux?
description: A look at the React Context API and whether you really need Redux for state management.

title: React for .NET Developers
description: Introducing React to .NET developers and how its approach to data flow and client-side UI can benefit ASP.NET applications.
